title: Did I Stutter?

jokes.text: Every time Michael's in a meeting, he makes me come in and give him a Post-it note telling him who's on the phone. I did it once and he freaked out. He loved it so much.
jokes.setup: Explaining Michael's phone protocol
jokes.punchline: Michael loved this minor task so much he demands it constantly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael characterization - his desperate need for importance manifesting in loving trivial office procedures

jokes.text: The thing is he doesn't get that many calls. So he has me make them up every 10 minutes.
jokes.setup: Michael loves the phone note system
jokes.punchline: He gets so few calls Pam has to fabricate them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that reveals both Michael's pathetic need for importance and Pam's enabling. Economy of revelation is excellent.

jokes.text: Ah, this is a very important client, but I have the most important client sitting right in front of me, my boss.
jokes.setup: Michael positioning himself as dedicated to customer service
jokes.punchline: Calls Ryan his most important 'client' when Ryan is his boss
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's confused understanding of corporate hierarchy and his desperate need to impress Ryan

jokes.text: Money isn't everything, Ryan. And you're my friend, and I don't want to be rude.
jokes.setup: Michael refusing to take an important call
jokes.punchline: Claiming friendship with his clearly uncomfortable boss
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael delusion about his relationship with superiors, creates appropriate cringe

jokes.text: Hiya, buddy.
jokes.setup: Michael finally takes the 'important client' call after all that fuss
jokes.punchline: Greets them with overly casual familiarity
jokes.explanation: Undermines his claims about the call's importance with inappropriately casual greeting

jokes.text: The company fired her for having the courage to augment her boobs.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining Jan's lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Describes breast enhancement as requiring 'courage'
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete misunderstanding of the situation, framing cosmetic surgery as heroic

jokes.text: Yes. Yes. Pattern. 'Pat-turn.' My friend Pat took a turn.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to memorize legal terms
jokes.punchline: Creates nonsensical mnemonic device
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Michael's childish learning methods and complete misunderstanding of how memory works

jokes.text: 'Dis-ray.' My friend Dis Ray got new specs.
jokes.setup: Continuing the mnemonic pattern
jokes.punchline: Even more absurd fake friend for 'disrespect'
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd mnemonic pattern, maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: Dis Ray Spect. My friend In-A-Pro drives a Prius with his behind neighbor.
jokes.setup: Mnemonic devices getting more elaborate
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical story about 'inappropriate behavior'
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity while showing Michael's complete inability to create coherent mnemonics

jokes.text: Does this work for you? Yep.
jokes.setup: Michael's completely insane mnemonic devices
jokes.punchline: Ryan's resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Ryan's defeated 'yep' perfectly captures his relationship with Michael's insanity

jokes.text: Million dollars. 4 million. $4 million! Man, that is a lot of guacamole! Lot of the green. Lot of green.
jokes.setup: Discussing lawsuit settlement
jokes.punchline: Reduces millions to 'guacamole' metaphor, gets excited about 'green'
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's childish understanding of money through food metaphor

jokes.text: Perfect crime!
jokes.setup: Describing his memorized testimony strategy
jokes.punchline: Calls legal preparation a 'perfect crime'
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete misunderstanding of legal proceedings and what constitutes a crime

jokes.text: Yeah, a bunch of rich lawyers took the bus.
jokes.setup: Correcting the lawyer joke setup
jokes.punchline: Misses the point entirely, focuses on transportation logistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion showing Michael completely missing the point of jokes while being pedantically 'correct'

jokes.text: Abso-fruitly.
jokes.setup: Michael agreeing to help the company
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining 'absolutely' and 'fruitly'
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael malapropism, shows his need to sound sophisticated while failing

jokes.text: My boyfriend is 12.
jokes.setup: Describing bringing Jim juice during ping pong
jokes.punchline: Reduces their relationship to childish caretaking
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and self-aware relationship humor. Pam's dry delivery makes this land well.

jokes.text: I don't talk trash. I talk smack. They're totally different. Trash talk is all hypothetical, like, 'Your mama's so fat', 'she could eat the Internet.' But smack talk is happening, like, right now. Like, 'You're ugly and I know it for a fact 'cause I got the evidence right there.'
jokes.setup: Kelly explaining her approach to competitive banter
jokes.punchline: Elaborate distinction ending with brutal directness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly - verbose, confident, and cutting. The theoretical vs. immediate distinction is genuinely clever and the examples escalate perfectly.

jokes.text: Your boyfriend is so weak, he needs steroids just to watch baseball.
jokes.setup: Kelly demonstrating her 'smack talk'
jokes.punchline: Absurd insult about needing steroids to watch sports
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of absurdity while maintaining Kelly's voice. The steroid joke is genuinely creative.

jokes.text: Jim couldn't hit a Ping-Pong ball if it was the size of the moon.
jokes.setup: More of Kelly's trash talk
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic impossibility - can't hit moon-sized ball
jokes.explanation: Good visual absurdity that maintains Kelly's escalating insult pattern

jokes.text: Were Jim's parents first cousins that were also bad at Ping-Pong?
jokes.setup: Kelly's insults getting more elaborate
jokes.punchline: Combines incest joke with ping-pong incompetence
jokes.explanation: Kelly's commitment to elaborate, specific insults. The specificity of 'also bad at ping-pong' elevates it.

jokes.text: Toby? Are you renewing your divorce vows before my deposition?
jokes.setup: Michael sees Toby at the deposition
jokes.punchline: Inverts 'wedding vows' to 'divorce vows'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael hostility toward Toby, with a decent wordplay twist on wedding terminology

jokes.text: People underestimate Michael. There are plenty of things that he is well above average at, like ice-skating. He is a very good ice-skater.
jokes.setup: Ryan defending Michael's competence
jokes.punchline: Only example is recreational ice-skating
jokes.explanation: Ryan's attempt to defend Michael accidentally reveals how little there is to defend. The specificity of ice-skating makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Awesome!
jokes.setup: Being told Jim needs him for travel receipts
jokes.punchline: Kevin's disproportionate enthusiasm for mundane task
jokes.explanation: Pure Kevin - finding genuine excitement in being included, even for boring work

jokes.text: I haven't actually seen it, but I have seen The Firm and I'm planning on renting The Pelican Brief.
jokes.setup: Being asked about knowing the plaintiff
jokes.punchline: Confuses 'plaintiff' with movies about legal cases
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection showing Michael's complete misunderstanding of legal terminology while revealing his movie-based legal knowledge

jokes.text: That's what she said.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asks if he was 'directly under her'
jokes.punchline: Michael's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate timing, but this is exactly when the catchphrase would be most damaging to his case

jokes.text: Well, delivery's all wrong. She's butchering it.
jokes.setup: Lawyer repeats his 'that's what she said' joke back
jokes.punchline: Michael critiques her comedic timing
jokes.explanation: Exceptional moment - Michael's wounded pride about his signature joke being poorly delivered in a legal setting is perfectly character-driven and meta

jokes.text: She thought it had to do with the twins. That's what I call them.
jokes.setup: Explaining what Jan thought caused her firing
jokes.punchline: Nicknames her breasts 'the twins'
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate euphemism, delivered with characteristic lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: To be delicate, they hang off milady's chest. They make milk.
jokes.setup: Asked to be more specific about 'the twins'
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical description while trying to be delicate
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at delicacy makes it worse, and 'milady' shows his misguided chivalry

jokes.text: Yes. And frankly, the timing was nothing short of predominant.
jokes.setup: Discussing the timing of Jan's firing
jokes.punchline: Misuses 'predominant' for 'suspicious' or 'coincidental'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism showing his attempt to sound intelligent backfiring

jokes.text: Well, it depends on how you define begin. I mean, if it's from the first time we shook hands, like, six years ago. If it's from the first time we kissed, it's, like, two years ago. If it was from the first time we kissed sober, it was, like, four months after that.
jokes.setup: Asked when his relationship with Jan began
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly compromising timeline details
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that destroys his case while perfectly capturing Michael's inability to stop talking and his complicated relationship with Jan

jokes.text: 'Mr. Scott, do you realize you just contradicted yourself?' 'I did.' 'Yes, you did.' 'Can I go to the bathroom?' 'No.' 'I really have to. I've been drinking lots of water.' 'You went five minutes ago.' 'That wasn't to go to the bathroom, that was to get out of a question.' 'You still have to answer it.' 'First, can I go to the bathroom?' 'No.'
jokes.setup: Michael asking for his 'line' like in a play
jokes.punchline: Pre-scripted dialogue revealing his childish avoidance tactics
jokes.explanation: Exceptional moment showing Michael's complete breakdown under pressure, with perfect escalation of childish excuses. The admission about bathroom breaks is devastatingly revealing.

jokes.text: Zoran Primorac, Jan-Ove Waldner, Wang Tao, Joerg Rosskopf and, of course, Ashraf Helmy. I even have a life-sized poster of Hugo Hoyama on my wall.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his ping-pong heroes
jokes.punchline: Rattles off obscure table tennis players with reverent detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - his obsessive knowledge of obscure sports figures and the life-sized poster detail

jokes.text: And the first time I left Pennsylvania was to go to the Hall of Fame induction ceremony of Andrzej Grubba.
jokes.setup: Continuing his ping-pong obsession
jokes.punchline: His first trip ever was for table tennis Hall of Fame
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing the depth of Dwight's obsession - making his first life trip about table tennis

jokes.text: 'Just got back from Jamaica, tan almost everywhere. Jan, almost everywhere. Hee, hee.'
jokes.setup: Reading Michael's diary entry
jokes.punchline: Childish wordplay about tanning and Jan
jokes.explanation: Pure cringe gold - Michael's juvenile diary humor being read in court

jokes.text: 'Jan was very specific that this is not going anywhere,' 'that it was a onetime mistake.' 'But we had sex six times, so you tell me.' 'I am definitely feeling very eerie.'
jokes.setup: Reading more diary entries
jokes.punchline: Michael's confused logic about 'one-time mistake' happening six times
jokes.explanation: Michael's diary logic is perfectly illogical - six times = one mistake. The confusion between 'eerie' and 'irie' adds layers

jokes.text: Could you make it 11?
jokes.setup: Lawyer ordering 10 copies of his diary
jokes.punchline: Michael wants an extra copy for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - even in his humiliation, he wants his diary back. Shows his complete lack of awareness of the damage

jokes.text: When I was a kid, my parents got divorced. They both wanted custody and they both asked me to testify against the other one in court. So, I don't know. I didn't want them to get divorced in the first place, you know? I love them both so much. I just wanted...
jokes.setup: Ryan trying to comfort Michael
jokes.punchline: Reveals his own traumatic childhood custody battle
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation that explains Ryan's personality while being simultaneously touching and disturbing

jokes.text: You e-mailed a topless photo of me to everyone in our company. Let's call it even.
jokes.setup: Michael upset about diary betrayal
jokes.punchline: Jan reveals Michael's much worse betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and perspective shift - Michael's complaint immediately dwarfed by his own worse behavior

jokes.text: Not a woman, just a cool, great-looking, best friend.
jokes.setup: Asked about Ryan being 'as hot as Jan'
jokes.punchline: Michael's description of Ryan as 'great-looking best friend'
jokes.explanation: Michael's homoerotic description of Ryan while trying to deflect, perfect character moment

jokes.text: Of water.
jokes.setup: Says Jan was drinking a lot during her divorce
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it was water, not alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - inadvertently implying alcoholism then over-correcting. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: 'I am out of carrots. I am out of sticks.' 'Mr. Scott has time and again proven himself' 'to be an unmanageable employee and a poor branch manager.' 'I recommend he be removed from that position' 'and reassigned to sales where he belongs.'
jokes.setup: Reading Jan's negative performance review
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of Michael's incompetence
jokes.explanation: The 'carrots and sticks' metaphor followed by devastating assessment creates perfect cringe

jokes.text: Well, he has gone from completely hopeless to simply miserable.
jokes.setup: Asked about Jim's ping-pong progress
jokes.punchline: Describes improvement as going from 'hopeless' to 'miserable'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight assessment - brutal honesty delivered as if it's encouraging progress

jokes.text: No, no, no, he works here, dumb ass!
jokes.setup: Jim thinking he needs to beat an outside client
jokes.punchline: Kelly's explosive reaction revealing the truth
jokes.explanation: Kelly's explosive frustration at Jim's confusion, perfect character reaction

jokes.text: See? I was number-one contender. I was being groomed.
jokes.setup: Hearing he was considered for Jan's job
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks this proves he was the top choice
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate need to find validation in being 'considered' shows his delusion

jokes.text: David Wallace, 'No.'
jokes.setup: Michael's excitement about being considered
jokes.punchline: Flat 'No' that he was never seriously considered
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Michael's hopes with ultimate economy - just 'No.'

jokes.text: Yes! Way to go! See that? Yeah. The floppy-haired girl you date won a point.
jokes.setup: Pam scores a point in ping-pong
jokes.punchline: Kelly's backhanded compliment calling Pam 'floppy-haired girl'
jokes.explanation: Kelly's inability to give a clean compliment, with the dismissive 'floppy-haired girl'

jokes.text: Hey, hey, you, you I don't like your boyfriend 'Cause, 'cause, 'cause, 'cause 'Cause he sucks at Ping-Pong
jokes.setup: Kelly taunting during the game
jokes.punchline: Parodies Avril Lavigne song with ping-pong lyrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly - turning pop culture into personal attack, very character-specific

jokes.text: Michael, I am very sorry. Oh, hey, no biggie. Just... No, no, no. This was rough. We never meant for you to get caught in the middle of this.
jokes.setup: Jan apologizing after the deposition disaster
jokes.punchline: Michael's pathetic attempt to minimize his humiliation
jokes.explanation: Michael's wounded pride trying to play it off as 'no biggie' when he's clearly devastated

jokes.text: I think you're a nice guy, too.
jokes.setup: David Wallace's condescending 'nice guy' comment from deposition
jokes.punchline: Michael's grateful response to what was actually dismissive
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Michael's complete inability to recognize condescension as insult

jokes.text: You expect to get screwed by your company, but you never expect to get screwed by your girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on the deposition
jokes.punchline: Philosophical insight about betrayal that's actually quite astute
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly wise Michael moment with universal truth about relationships vs. work expectations

jokes.text: How about Chinese? We should really try to save some money. How about something cheap? That was my cheap suggestion. Chinese was my cheap suggestion.
jokes.setup: Discussing dinner after their legal defeat
jokes.punchline: Michael's defensive explanation that Chinese was already his budget option
jokes.explanation: Shows their relationship dynamic - Michael's hurt pride over suggesting something already considered cheap

tmdb_overview: Pam is sporting a new look at work with her glasses. She admits she spent the night at a "friend's" house and forgot her contact solution. Michael makes sure to point out that the glasses make her look ugly, and if she wants to be hot, the glasses need to come off.
